hero.subtitle: Payroll drinks straight from the HRIS: compensation profiles, hours and absences are already in. You prepare the pay run, review it, it gets approved and payslips go out — with separation of duties and country-by-country compliance.

honesty.does: A management calculation engine (gross = base + components + adjustments), pay runs with a state cycle and approval, generated payslips and accounting exports in CSV.
honesty.not: No bank integration and no bank file submission. Country-specific legal withholdings are in preview (see packs). We only claim what already works.

motor.body: You create the pay run with a period and an entity, and the engine assembles every line: the current salary profile, fixed components and the month's adjustments — hour-bank payouts, one-off corrections and unpaid absences. Management arithmetic, transparent and explainable.
checks: Hour-bank "payout" comes in as an adjustment
One pay run = one entity = one country

countries.label: Generic
countries.note: Generic pack · net = gross, no legal withholdings.

corridas.body: Every pay run moves through a state cycle with separation of duties: hr_admin prepares and reviews; only the owner approves and marks it paid. Every transition lands in the audit log, with actor and date.

roles.name: OWNER
roles.scope: Approves the run · marks it paid

roles.name: HR ADMIN
roles.scope: Creates, generates lines, reviews and adjusts

modulos.receiptsDesc: When the run is approved, everyone's payslips are generated. Check them from the profile or from the run — no rebuilding them by hand.
modulos.compDesc: Salary, components and an effective-dated history live on the employee record. Payroll always reads the current profile; the payroll roster shows who has it set up.

packs.intro: Your company's base country sets currency, holidays and rules. The generic pack already works; legal packs keep landing — and until they do, we say it plainly.

cards.name: Generic
cards.desc: Pure management: gross = base + components + adjustments. Net = gross, no withholdings.

cards.name: Spain
cards.desc: Legal withholdings for Spain as base country.

cards.name: Venezuela
cards.desc: Contributions and benefits with dual VES/USD currency.

cards.name: Brazil
cards.desc: Employer charges and provisions under the Brazilian regime.

packs.exports: Real exports: payroll summary and accounting entry in CSV, available after approval. The bank file and local compliance sit behind the country pack (mocked with a badge until it's live).

cta.body: Start free with the generic pack. Turn on your country when its legal compliance ships — nothing to migrate.
